content:
# Digital Rock Physics

**Summary**: Using high-resolution images of rock samples to model and simulate petrophysical properties.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2024-alabbad|Alabbad, 2024]] employed digital-rock-physics to quantify the relationship between kerogen, porosity, and surface area.

- [[2025-refaat|Refaat, 2025]] applied digital-rock-physics-modeling to quantify the volumes of total and connected pores.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2025-refaat|Refaat, 2025]] applied digital-rock-physics-modeling to quantify the volumes of total and connected pores.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- FIB-SEM imaging revealed a 'flipped V' shape in the kerogen-porosity relationship as an indicator of maturity. ([[2024-alabbad|Alabbad, 2024, p. 137]])

- Allowed for the calculation of pore volume, area, and equivalent diameter for individual pores. ([[2025-refaat|Refaat, 2025, p. 49]])
